items.text:
Subject: Study Group for Chemistry 101 Final Exam

Hi everyone,

I'm organizing a study group to prepare for the Chemistry 101 final exam on December 15. We'll meet in the Library, Room 204, every Tuesday and Thursday from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM, starting November 1. Each member needs to contribute $5.00 to cover printing costs for practice tests and handouts.

Please bring your textbook, notes, and a calculator to each session. I'll also prepare a review packet, but you must email me your specific questions by October 25 so I can include them. We have limited spots, so please RSVP by November 1.

Thanks,
Maria Chen

format_metadata.subject: Study Group for Chemistry 101 Final Exam

options.A: Meet in Room 204 every Monday and Wednesday
options.C: Purchase a new textbook before the first session
options.D: Attend a review session on December 15

questions.explanation: The email states, 'Each member needs to contribute $5.00 to cover printing costs for practice tests and handouts.'

options.A: It has a limited number of available spots
options.B: It will meet only once before the final exam
options.C: It requires students to bring their own handouts
options.D: It is open to all students without registration

questions.explanation: The email says, 'We have limited spots, so please RSVP by November 1,' implying that not everyone can join.

options.A: To announce the Chemistry 101 final exam date
options.B: To request donations for printing materials
options.C: To invite students to join a study group
options.D: To provide instructions for submitting questions

questions.explanation: The email is primarily about organizing a study group, as shown by the subject line and the invitation to participate.

items.text:
Notice: Wi-Fi Security Certificate Update

Date: October 10

Information Technology Services will update the campus Wi-Fi security certificate on November 1 from 2:00 AM to 5:00 AM. During this time, wireless internet access will be temporarily unavailable in all academic buildings.

To ensure a smooth transition:
- All students must install the new certificate by October 25. A $5.00 late fee will apply for installations after this date.
- The certificate can be downloaded from the IT portal at it.campus.edu.
- Devices running outdated operating systems may not be compatible.

For assistance, contact the IT Help Desk in Anderson Hall, Room 12, or call 555-0100.

Thank you for your cooperation.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students do to avoid a late fee?

options.A: Install the new certificate by October 25
options.B: Update their operating system before November 1
options.C: Visit the IT Help Desk in Anderson Hall

questions.explanation: The notice states, 'All students must install the new certificate by October 25. A $5.00 late fee will apply for installations after this date.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Wi-Fi update?

options.A: It will affect only academic buildings
options.B: It requires students to use a specific device
options.C: It may cause connectivity issues for some devices

questions.explanation: The notice mentions 'Devices running outdated operating systems may not be compatible,' implying potential problems for some users.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a temporary Wi-Fi outage
options.B: To inform students about a security certificate update
options.C: To warn about late fees for IT services
options.D: To promote the IT Help Desk

questions.explanation: The notice primarily addresses the Wi-Fi security certificate update, including deadlines and steps for installation.

items.text:
Notice: Campus Mailroom Holiday Hours

Date: December 1

The campus mailroom will operate on reduced hours during the winter break.

- December 20 to December 24: Open 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM
- December 25 to January 2: CLOSED
- January 3 to January 5: Open 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM
- Regular hours (8:00 AM to 6:00 PM) resume on January 6.

Packages not picked up by December 19 will be subject to a $2.00 per day storage fee. To avoid this fee, please collect all items before the holiday closure.

For urgent inquiries, contact mailroom@campus.edu or call 555-0200.

Thank you, and happy holidays!

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students do to avoid a storage fee?

options.C: Contact the mailroom for urgent inquiries

questions.explanation: The notice states, 'Packages not picked up by December 19 will be subject to a $2.00 per day storage fee. To avoid this fee, please collect all items before the holiday closure.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the mailroom's schedule?

options.A: It will be open every day during the winter break
options.B: It will be completely closed for over a week
options.C: It will have extended hours on December 20
options.D: It will resume regular hours on January 3

questions.explanation: The notice indicates the mailroom is closed from December 25 to January 2, which is a period of 9 days.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a new storage fee policy
options.B: To remind students to collect packages
options.C: To inform about holiday hours and fees
options.D: To provide contact information for the mailroom

questions.explanation: The notice focuses on the mailroom's holiday hours and associated storage fees, aiming to inform and guide students.

items.text:
Subject: Invitation to the Annual Cultural Festival

Dear students,

The International Student Association invites you to our Annual Cultural Festival on March 20, from 11:00 AM to 6:00 PM in the Student Union Plaza. This event features food from 15 countries, live music, and dance performances.

Tickets are $5.00 in advance and $8.00 at the door. You can purchase them at the Student Activities Office, Room 101, until March 18. Volunteers who help set up or clean up will receive free entry and a T-shirt. Please sign up by March 10 if you are interested.

We hope to see you there!

Best regards,
Luis Ramirez
President, International Student Association

format_metadata.subject: Invitation to the Annual Cultural Festival

questions.stem: According to the email, what must volunteers do to receive free entry?

options.A: Pay $5.00 for a ticket in advance
options.B: Sign up by March 10 to help set up or clean up
options.D: Purchase a T-shirt from the Student Activities Office

questions.explanation: The email states, 'Volunteers who help set up or clean up will receive free entry and a T-shirt. Please sign up by March 10 if you are interested.'

questions.stem: What is the cost of a ticket purchased at the door?
questions.explanation: The email says, 'Tickets are $5.00 in advance and $8.00 at the door.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Cultural Festival?

options.A: It will take place in a different location each year
options.B: It offers a discount for early ticket purchases
options.C: It requires all attendees to volunteer
options.D: It is organized by the Student Activities Office

questions.explanation: The email mentions tickets are cheaper in advance ($5.00) than at the door ($8.00), implying a discount for early purchase.
